beats.narration_text: Vanakkam. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of spherical harmonics & angular momentum cone. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of spherical harmonics & angular momentum cone

beats.narration_text: Spherical Harmonics & Angular Momentum Cone is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. Quantum angular momentum quantisation — cone precession & |Y. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
beats.new_visual_element: Concept card: Spherical Harmonics & Angular Momentum Cone

beats.narration_text: Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Spherical Harmonics & Angular Momentum Cone. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: lSlider, mSlider. Verify: Quantum angular momentum quantisation — cone precession & |Y. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Spherical Harmonics & Angular Momentum Cone. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: lSlider, mSlider. Verify: Q

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.code_snippet: // Spherical Harmonics & Angular Momentum Cone — core physics
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)
beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from 05-spherical-harmonics-cone.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: spherical harmonics & angular momentum cone. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag main slider from 0 to 4. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of main slider from 0 to 4

beats.narration_text: Quantum angular momentum quantisation — cone precession & |Y. Exactly as the equations predict.

One prompt. A live demonstration of spherical harmonics & angular momentum cone. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Spherical Harmonics & Angular Momentum Cone simulation and add a second slider that controls the main parameter. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
